name: 49 · xArm Digital Twin (live joint stream from real feedback)
description: Drives a REAL xArm 6 through a multi-waypoint out-and-back FollowJointTrajectory action AND streams the arm's ACTUAL joint feedback as a data channel so the UI animates a live digital twin of the real motion on the graph edge — NO re-computed/interpolated trajectory. The single `move` node does `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`), commanding home → [0, -0.3, -0.3, 0, 0.6, 0] → home over ~9 s on the real (fake-hardware) arm (reusing demo 32's `interface_typedefs`/`xarm_fleet` capacity + `ros.robot_model == xarm6` requirement verbatim). The SAME node declares a Data/Out channel `joints`: as the controller follows the trajectory the ROS backend streams each ACTUAL feedback frame (`actual.positions`) as one NDJSON line `{joint_names, positions}` (content_type `application/vnd.aithericon.joint-state+x-ndjson;model=xarm6`) onto the channel. The UI render registry (planLiveRender) routes that stream to the robot-arm twin renderer, drawing a live 3D xArm 6 on the `move` → `twin_view` edge that animates the arm's TRUE joint states in real time. The `twin_view` node is the data-plane consumer that drains the channel (`for chunk in stream('joints')`), counts complete frames, and feeds End, which maps `frames = {{ twin_view.received_frames }}`. The bulk joint stream rides the out-of-band JetStream datastream transport — it NEVER enters the net marking (open + close only). LIVE ONLY: requires `just dev xarm-up` to build the xArm 6 + rosbridge container and enroll an `xarm_fleet` runner advertising `ros.robot_model == xarm6`.
